digest.component_or_area: op-cert trusted gate service AWS deploy (Terraform + compose/nginx)
propositions: Declare in Terraform and actually deploy the op-cert trusted gate service on the existing rebar-gerrit box behind an API Gateway HTTP API with zero increase in fixed monthly cost.
API Gateway routes use AWS_IAM (SigV4) auth over TLS, with execute-api:Invoke granted only to the IaC-created rebar-opcert-admin role whose trust policy comes from opcert_admin_principal_arns.
Integration targets the host-nginx https origin injecting a static X-Opcert-Guard header, and nginx fail-closed (403) rejects /opcert/ requests lacking a matching guard value.

Store the passphrase-free Ed25519 private key and the origin-guard value as SSM SecureString parameters under the existing rebar-gerrit-ssm-params-read grant, with lifecycle ignore_changes only on the operator-seeded key parameter.

Wire the materialize-opcert-guard.sh script into compose-up.sh before docker compose up, rendering the guard SSM value into the nginx map and reloading host nginx, with a documented fail-closed rotation runbook.

Prove the deployed endpoint end-to-end: a SigV4-signed job round-trip succeeds, an unsigned request is rejected at API Gateway, a direct-to-origin request without the guard header is rejected, and the environment key/env_id is pinned in trusted_environments.yaml.
